_note: Paper-derived figures carry the paper citation. Live NBA-stat cohort/dashboard values are PENDING (NBA.com/stats is not CSV-exportable and player tracking is not available for all games). Do not estimate.

contract.value: 3-year / $39M extension, ~$13M average annual value; 2026-27 cap figure ~$13.0M

led_oreb.value: Led the NBA in offensive rebounds in 2021-22 and 2022-23

paper_ballscreens_2016.source: McIntyre, Brooks, Guttag & Wiens — 'Recognizing and Analyzing Ball Screen Defense in the NBA', SSAC 2016 (per ESPN conference write-up)

paper_adams_ppp_over.value: Fisher+Adams and Jackson+Adams: 0.00 PPP allowed going 'over' the screen (n=14 and n=20 — small samples, state honestly)

paper_adams_quote_2016.value: "Steven Adams is a standout defender; regardless of who he is paired with, Adams and his teammate are particularly effective at blocking the defense from scoring off of a pick and roll."
paper_adams_quote_2016.note: The one long quote of the piece.

paper_adams_sd_2016.value: Figure 8 places Adams 2+ standard deviations better than league average

paper_oreb_value_2026.value: Offensive rebounds valued at +2.31 expected points (vs. the traditional +1.0 weight; up to +3.9 at max leverage)
paper_oreb_value_2026.source: Jenkins — 'Deep Reinforcement Learning for NBA Player Valuation', SSAC 2026
paper_oreb_value_2026.note: Present as that paper's estimate, not consensus.

paper_secondchance_ppp_2025.source: Stephanos — 'Enhancing NBA True Shot Charts', SSAC 2025

paper_recovery_split_2025.value: Close-range misses recovered 38.7% vs 21.0% for mid-range misses

paper_recovery_gradient_2012.source: Maheswaran, Chang, Henehan & Danesis — 'Deconstructing the Rebound with Optical Tracking Data', SSAC 2012

paper_rebound_geometry_2012.value: Rebounds are secured below 8 ft; 97.5% of rebound opportunities land within ~14 ft of the rim; 'the value of boxing out' named as future work

paper_open_shot_2014.value: 19,386 three-pointers: open (nearest defender ≥6 ft) ≈ 40% vs pressured ≈ 32%; a defender within 6 ft even 0.6s before release → ~27%
paper_open_shot_2014.source: Lucey, Bialkowski, Carr, Yue & Matthews — 'How to Get an Open Shot', SSAC 2014

paper_move_or_die_2015.value: Raw movement totals don't correlate with success; movement's value is unpredictability — an entropy measure correlates with future open looks
paper_move_or_die_2015.source: D'Amour, Cervone, Bornn & Goldsberry — 'Move or Die', SSAC 2015

paper_assist_quality.value: Pass characteristics significantly affect makes only at touch times under ~2 seconds — quick-touch passing is where hidden value lives

paper_dwight_efficiency_2013.value: Interior defense as efficiency suppression: league ~49.7% close-range with a big within 5 ft of the rim; ~38% vs Hibbert / Sanders (2011–13)

paper_dwight_frequency_2013.value: Frequency suppression: 57.2% of attempts at the rim league-wide → 48.2% with Howard protecting it; 'invisible blocks' (2011–13)

paper_proximity_causal_2023.value: Defender proximity causally lowers make probability, strongest near the rim
paper_proximity_causal_2023.source: Tenan & Rezai — 'Player Tracking Facilitates Valid Causal Inference', SSAC 2023

paper_roi_2025.value: A contract = invested capital returning per-game cash flows; missed games are 'defaults'
paper_roi_2025.source: Lautier — 'A New Framework to Estimate ROI for Player Salaries', SSAC 2025

chart1_oreb_scatter.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Tracking → Rebounding: OREB, OREB Chances, Adjusted OREB Chance%, Contested OREB — for the center cohort (Adams, Gobert, Claxton, Zubac, Hartenstein, Kessler + others, USG% < ~18, min-qualified)

chart2_boxouts.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Hustle: Box Outs + team-rebound-on-box-out conversion

chart3_screen_ladder.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Hustle: Screen Assists per 36 — low-usage-big cohort ladder

chart4_passing_tiles.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Tracking → Passing: Potential AST, Secondary AST, FT AST, AST-to-Pass% Adj

chart5_opp_splits.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Defense Dashboard: opponent FG% by zone (close/mid/3) + rim FG <6ft, Adams on vs off

chart6_twoman.fetch: NBA.com/stats → Lineups (2-man): Adams+Sengun, Adams+Houston guards net ratings

chart7_salary_quadrant.fetch: Spotrac salaries + composite public value-proxy for the center cohort
